plan.name: Lambda Labs Single-GPU Training E2E Verification

context.summary: Verify end-to-end training pipeline on Lambda Labs with MLflow artifact upload. Training itself WORKS (verified 2x on A100). The blocking issue was MLflow artifact upload failing because server was v2.20.3 while client was v3.10.0. Server has been upgraded to v3.10.0 + multipart upload enabled. Need ONE more clean run to verify the 910 MB .pth checkpoint uploads successfully.

file.purpose: Lambda SkyPilot YAML with Docker image + DVC pull
Multi-region launcher with retry (17 regions, EU first)
Pulumi stack for UpCloud MLflow server (line 240-280)
All credentials (Lambda API key, GHCR token, MLflow, DVC)
Root cause analysis of artifact failure
Why RunPod doesn't work for Docker

context.lambda-gpu-availability: Lambda single-GPU instances are frequently sold out across all 17 regions. The multi-region launcher (scripts/launch_smoke_test.py) handles this with retry_until_up=True. GPU types tried: A10 ($0.86) → A100 ($1.48) → GH200 ($1.99) → H100 ($2.86). Capacity appears intermittently — the A100 was available twice during the session.

task.title: Launch smoke test on Lambda
task.description: Launch sam3_vanilla smoke test on Lambda with retry_until_up. Wait for single-GPU capacity to appear.
task.command: uv run python scripts/launch_smoke_test.py --model sam3_vanilla --cloud lambda
task.success-criteria: - Cluster status transitions to UP - Training flow starts (Prefect flow run appears in logs) - SAM3 model weights load (1468/1468 params) - MLflow experiment created on remote server

task.title: Verify training completion
task.description: Monitor training for 2 epochs, 1 fold, 4 volumes on Lambda GPU. Expected: ~10-15 min on A10, ~9 min on A100.
task.success-criteria: - metric_history.json contains epoch 0 metrics - best_val_loss.pth saved (~910 MB) - epoch_latest.pth + last.pth saved - train_loss and val_loss logged

task.title: CRITICAL: Verify MLflow artifact upload
task.description: THE WHOLE POINT. Verify that the 910 MB .pth checkpoint uploads to MLflow via the multipart upload proxy (server v3.10.0 + MLFLOW_ENABLE_PROXY_MULTIPART_UPLOAD=true). Previous failures: HTTP 500 from server v2.20.3 (no multipart support).

commands.cmd: ssh minivess-smoke-test "grep -E 'too many 500|Failed to upload|artifact' ~/sky_logs/*/run.log | tail -10"

task.success-criteria: - ZERO "too many 500 error" in logs - ZERO "Failed to upload checkpoint artifact" in logs - MLflow API shows artifacts for the run ID - checkpoints/best_val_loss.pth appears in MLflow artifact store

if-fails.diagnosis: 1. Check MLflow server logs: ssh deploy@185.20.139.158 "sudo docker logs minivess-mlflow --tail 50" 2. Check if multipart is actually being used (look for "multipart" in server logs) 3. Check UpCloud S3 bucket directly for uploaded chunks 4. Verify server version: sudo docker exec minivess-mlflow python3 -c "import mlflow; print(mlflow.__version__)" 5. Check MLFLOW_ENABLE_PROXY_MULTIPART_UPLOAD on server: grep MULTIPART /opt/mlflow/docker-compose.yml
if-fails.fallback: If multipart upload still fails on UpCloud, the issue is the small VPS (2vCPU/4GB) or UpCloud S3 compatibility. Next step: move to GCP (docs/planning/lambda-mlflow-failure-analysis.md).

task.title: Test all 3 models
task.description: After sam3_vanilla verified, test sam3_hybrid and vesselfm.

commands.cmd: uv run python scripts/launch_smoke_test.py --model sam3_hybrid --cloud lambda
uv run python scripts/launch_smoke_test.py --model vesselfm --cloud lambda

task.title: Update Pulumi template
task.description: Ensure deployment/pulumi/__main__.py has v3.10.0 + all env vars so future Pulumi deployments are correct from the start. Pin MLFLOW_SERVER_VERSION in .env.example as single source of truth.

transition-to-gcp.when: After Lambda Labs E2E is verified (L3 passes), OR if Lambda capacity remains unavailable and user creates GCP account.
transition-to-gcp.steps: 1. User creates GCP account ($300 free credits) 2. gcloud auth login + sky check gcp 3. Create Pulumi stack: deployment/pulumi/gcp/__main__.py 4. Deploy: Cloud Run (MLflow) + Cloud SQL + GCS + Artifact Registry 5. Update SkyPilot YAML: cloud: gcp, use_spot: true, L4/T4/A100 6. Test with MOUNT_CACHED checkpointing for spot auto-resume 7. Simulate preemption: gcloud compute instances simulate-maintenance-event
